_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Ability to distinguish between correlation and causation in analytical findings, communicating the distinction and its implications for decision-making clearly to business audiences.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI AB domain. Ability AB-A-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-K-009 (AB, cosine: 0.457), AI-K-002 (AI, cosine: 0.437).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 1.9/10 (raw 0.187). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. NICE-only terms [biases, cognitive, knowledge] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, analytical, audiences, business, causation, clearly] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.861 (P=0.875, R=0.848). P exceeds R by 0.027 — the NICE element's 4 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 22 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Knowledge of cognitive biases" and "Ability to distinguish between correlation and causation in analytical findings," are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.482 > STS 0.187 (gap +0.295). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. NICE terms: [biases, cognitive, knowledge]. WIDAI terms: [ability, analytical, audiences, business, causation, clearly].
